q: Life reflects your own thoughts back to you.
h:
“Life reflects your own thoughts back to you.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: The search for happiness is one of the chief sources of unhappiness.
h:
“The search for happiness is one of the chief sources of unhappiness.” —
Eric Hoffer

q: To be alive - is Power.
h:
“To be alive - is Power.” —
Emily Dickinson

q: Thinking is difficult, that's why most people judge.
h:
“Thinking is difficult, that's why most people judge.” —
Carl Jung

q: Wherever you are, and whatever you do, be in love.
h:
“Wherever you are, and whatever you do, be in love.” —
Rumi

q: People do not decide their futures, they decide their habits and their habits decide their futures.
h:
“People do not decide their futures, they decide their habits and their habits decide their futures.” —
Gary Keller

q: The happiest people in the world are those who feel absolutely terrific about themselves.
h:
“The happiest people in the world are those who feel absolutely terrific about themselves.” —
Brian Tracy

q: Just as true humor is laughter at oneself, true humanity is knowledge of oneself.
h:
“Just as true humor is laughter at oneself, true humanity is knowledge of oneself.” —
Alan Watts

q: People are weird. When we find someone with weirdness that is compatible with ours, we team up and call it love.
h:
“People are weird. When we find someone with weirdness that is compatible with ours, we team up and call it love.” —
Dr. Seuss

q: My actions are my only true belongings. I cannot escape the consequences of my actions. My actions are the ground upon which I stand.
h:
“My actions are my only true belongings. I cannot escape the consequences of my actions. My actions are the ground upon which I stand.” —
Thich Nhat Hanh

q: To be fully alive, fully human, and completely awake is to be continually thrown out of the nest.
h:
“To be fully alive, fully human, and completely awake is to be continually thrown out of the nest.” —
Pema Chodron

q: An inventor is a man who asks Why? of the universe and lets nothing stand between the answer and his mind.
h:
“An inventor is a man who asks Why? of the universe and lets nothing stand between the answer and his mind.” —
Ayn Rand

q: We crave for new sensations but soon become indifferent to them. The wonders of yesterday are today common occurrences
h:
“We crave for new sensations but soon become indifferent to them. The wonders of yesterday are today common occurrences ” —
Nikola Tesla

q: You can do two things at once, but you can't focus effectively on two things at once.
h:
“You can do two things at once, but you can't focus effectively on two things at once.” —
Gary Keller

q: Always show more kindness than seems necessary because the person receiving it needs it more than you will ever know.
h:
“Always show more kindness than seems necessary because the person receiving it needs it more than you will ever know.” —
Colin Powell

q: Where does a thought go when it's forgotten?
h:
“Where does a thought go when it's forgotten?” —
Sigmund Freud

q: When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on.
h:
“When you reach the end of your rope, tie a knot in it and hang on.” —
Franklin D. Roosevelt

q: Riches don't make a man rich, they only make him busier.
h:
“Riches don't make a man rich, they only make him busier.” —
Christopher Columbus

q: I must also have a dark side if I am to be whole.
h:
“I must also have a dark side if I am to be whole.” —
Carl Jung

q: The first thing you learn in life is you're a fool. The last thing you learn in life is you're the same fool.
h:
“The first thing you learn in life is you're a fool. The last thing you learn in life is you're the same fool. ” —
Ray Bradbury

q: He who leaves the game wins it.
h:
“He who leaves the game wins it.” —
Nicolas Chamfort

q: Most people spend more time and energy going around problems than in trying to solve them.
h:
“Most people spend more time and energy going around problems than in trying to solve them. ” —
Henry Ford

q: Instead of being intimidated by the limitations, be inspired to find new ways around them.
h:
“Instead of being intimidated by the limitations, be inspired to find new ways around them.” —
Ralph Marston

q: The most important battle is one to conquer yourself.
h:
“The most important battle is one to conquer yourself.” —
Yanni

q: It's easier to lose than to win.
h:
“It's easier to lose than to win. ” —
Wayne Gretzky

q: Stress happens when the mind resists what is.
h:
“Stress happens when the mind resists what is.” —
Dan Millman

q: You can do nothing to change the past, and the future will never come exactly as you plan or hope for.
h:
“You can do nothing to change the past, and the future will never come exactly as you plan or hope for.” —
Dan Millman

q: Freedom lies in being bold.
h:
“Freedom lies in being bold.” —
Robert Frost

q: Fears are nothing more than a state of mind.
h:
“Fears are nothing more than a state of mind.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: You are free, and that is why you are lost.
h:
“You are free, and that is why you are lost.” —
Franz Kafka

q: All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.
h:
“All that we see or seem is but a dream within a dream.” —
Edgar Allan Poe

q: It is indeed a radical act of love just to sit down and be quiet for a time by yourself.
h:
“It is indeed a radical act of love just to sit down and be quiet for a time by yourself.” —
Jon Kabat-Zinn

q: Don't worry about being successful, but work toward being significant and the success will naturally follow.
h:
“Don't worry about being successful, but work toward being significant and the success will naturally follow.” —
Oprah Winfrey

q: You cannot have a positive life and a negative mind.
h:
“You cannot have a positive life and a negative mind.” —
Joyce Meyer

q: If we learn to open our hearts, anyone, including the people who drive us crazy, can be our teacher.
h:
“If we learn to open our hearts, anyone, including the people who drive us crazy, can be our teacher.” —
Pema Chodron

q: Every day passes whether you participate or not.
h:
“Every day passes whether you participate or not.” —
Ming-Dao Deng

q: It isn't what you do, but how you do it.
h:
“It isn't what you do, but how you do it.” —
John Wooden

q: Trust is the foundation of great leadership.
h:
“Trust is the foundation of great leadership.” —
Lolly Daskal

q: Life is a question and how we live it is our answer.
h:
“Life is a question and how we live it is our answer.” —
Gary Keller

q: You'll get more from being a peacemaker than a warrior.
h:
“You'll get more from being a peacemaker than a warrior.” —
Arnold Schwarzenegger

q: Every moment of happiness requires a great amount of Ignorance
h:
“Every moment of happiness requires a great amount of Ignorance” —
Honore de Balzac

q: I was wise enough to never grow up while fooling most people into believing I had.
h:
“I was wise enough to never grow up while fooling most people into believing I had. ” —
Margaret Mead

q: There is no excuse for not trying.
h:
“There is no excuse for not trying.” —
Barack Obama

q: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
h:
“Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.” —
Robert Collier

q: Words are the clothes thoughts wear.
h:
“Words are the clothes thoughts wear.” —
Samuel Beckett

q: Life is not meant to be easy my child, but take courage: it can be delightful.
h:
“Life is not meant to be easy my child, but take courage: it can be delightful.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: I am always doing that which I can not do, in order that I may learn how to do it.
h:
“I am always doing that which I can not do, in order that I may learn how to do it.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: Many people will walk in and out of your life, but only true friends will leave footprints in your heart.
h:
“Many people will walk in and out of your life, but only true friends will leave footprints in your heart.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: Courage is knowing what not to fear.
h:
“Courage is knowing what not to fear.” —
Plato

q: Take responsibility of your own happiness, never put it in other people's hands.
h:
“Take responsibility of your own happiness, never put it in other people's hands.” —
Roy T. Bennett
